model.dbt_smoke.week3_extractions.name: week3_extractions

lineage_notes: Consumer-side lineage enrichment was expanded using registry-aligned snapshots in addition to the primary producer snapshot.

model.dbt_smoke.week3_extractions.description: dbt-compatible counterpart for generated contract week3-extractions built from outputs/week3/extractions.jsonl.

doc_id.name: doc_id
doc_id.description: Primary identifier for the extracted document record.

source_path.name: source_path
source_path.description: Original source path or URL for the document.

source_hash.name: source_hash
source_hash.description: SHA-256 content hash of the source document.

entities_count.name: entities_count
entities_count.description: Number of entities linked to the document record.

extraction_model.name: extraction_model
extraction_model.description: Extraction strategy or model label used to produce the record.

processing_time_ms.name: processing_time_ms
processing_time_ms.description: End-to-end extraction processing time in milliseconds.

token_count_input.name: token_count_input
token_count_input.description: Approximate input token count used by the extraction workflow.

token_count_output.name: token_count_output
token_count_output.description: Approximate output token count produced by the extraction workflow.

extracted_at.name: extracted_at
extracted_at.description: Timestamp when the extraction record was produced.

fact_fact_id.name: fact_fact_id
fact_fact_id.description: Primary identifier for the extracted fact.

fact_entity_refs_count.name: fact_entity_refs_count
fact_entity_refs_count.description: Number of entity references linked from the extracted fact.

fact_entity_refs.name: fact_entity_refs
fact_entity_refs.description: Entity identifiers referenced by the extracted fact.

fact_confidence.name: fact_confidence
fact_confidence.description: Confidence value for the extracted fact. This must remain in the 0.0-1.0 range.

fact_page_ref.name: fact_page_ref
fact_page_ref.description: Source page number associated with the extracted fact.

fact_source_excerpt.name: fact_source_excerpt
fact_source_excerpt.description: Supporting excerpt copied from the source document for the extracted fact.

lineage_notes: Consumer-side lineage enrichment was expanded using registry-aligned snapshots in addition to the primary producer snapshot.
Consumer-side lineage enrichment was expanded using registry-aligned snapshots in addition to the primary producer snapshot.

model.dbt_smoke.week3_extractions.raw_code:
select
  doc_id,
  source_path,
  source_hash,
  entities_count,
  extraction_model,
  processing_time_ms,
  token_count_input,
  token_count_output,
  replace(cast(extracted_at as varchar), ' ', 'T') as extracted_at,
  fact_fact_id,
  fact_text,
  fact_entity_refs_count,
  fact_entity_refs,
  fact_confidence,
  fact_page_ref,
  fact_source_excerpt
from {{ ref('week3_extractions_flat') }}

macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__list_relations_.macro_sql:
{% macro duckdb__list_relations_without_caching(schema_relation) %}
  {% call statement('list_relations_without_caching', fetch_result=True) -%}
    select
      '{{ schema_relation.database }}' as database,
      table_name as name,
      table_schema as schema,
      CASE table_type
        WHEN 'BASE TABLE' THEN 'table'
        WHEN 'VIEW' THEN 'view'
        WHEN 'LOCAL TEMPORARY' THEN 'table'
        END as type
    from system.information_schema.tables
    where lower(table_schema) = '{{ schema_relation.schema | lower }}'
    and lower(table_catalog) = '{{ schema_relation.database | lower }}'
  {% endcall %}
  {{ return(load_result('list_relations_without_caching').table) }}
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__get_catalog.name: duckdb__get_catalog
macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__get_catalog.macro_sql:
{% macro duckdb__get_catalog(information_schema, schemas) -%}
  {%- call statement('catalog', fetch_result=True) -%}
    with relations AS (
      select
        t.table_name
        , t.database_name
        , t.schema_name
        , 'BASE TABLE' as table_type
        , t.comment as table_comment
      from duckdb_tables() t
      WHERE t.database_name = '{{ database }}'
      UNION ALL
      SELECT v.view_name as table_name
      , v.database_name
      , v.schema_name
      , 'VIEW' as table_type
      , v.comment as table_comment
      from duckdb_views() v
      WHERE v.database_name = '{{ database }}'
    )
    select
        '{{ database }}' as table_database,
        r.schema_name as table_schema,
        r.table_name,
        r.table_type,
        r.table_comment,
        c.column_name,
        c.column_index as column_index,
        c.data_type as column_type,
        c.comment as column_comment,
        NULL as table_owner
    FROM relations r JOIN duckdb_columns() c ON r.schema_name = c.schema_name AND r.table_name = c.table_name
    WHERE (
        {%- for schema in schemas -%}
          upper(r.schema_name) = upper('{{ schema }}'){%- if not loop.last %} or {% endif -%}
        {%- endfor -%}
    )
    ORDER BY
        r.schema_name,
        r.table_name,
        c.column_index
  {%- endcall -%}
  {{ return(load_result('catalog').table) }}
{%- endmacro %}

doc.dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--select` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- [What is dbt](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/introduction)?
- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [dbt Community](https://www.getdbt.com/community/) for questions and discussion
